rows.Event Name: Free Wellness On The Green Event
rows.Event Description: Wellness on the Lawn! April 25, 9AM–12PM | FREE Fitness classes, cold plunge, sauna, mimosas, IV bar & giveaways. Free + family-friendly Join us at Desert Ridge Marketplace for the Wellness on the Green Wellness Event on the AMC Lawn from 9AM-12PM for a morning of wellness, movement, and relaxation. Experience multiple FREE fitness classes, including classes from Reformed Pilates, PVOLVE( Jennifer Anisten's fitness company), refresh in our cold plunge and sauna, sip mimosas at our mobile Sip & Stroll bar, and boost your energy at the on-site IV station. Plus, enjoy giveaways, wellness-inspired surprises, and more as you recharge. This free, family-friendly event is the perfect way to glow, flow, and feel your best – don’t miss it!

FOUND:RE Contemporary and FOUND:RE Phoenix Hotel announce “Tiny,” an exhibition featuring local artists with works 8” x 8” or smaller. “Tiny” is explored through a variety of mediums such as painting, drawing, sculpture, photography, fiber art, printmaking, and more. This exhibition features artwork as little as 1” x 1” to as large as 8” x 8”. Visitors are sure to find something to delight them! “Tiny” features the work of Lando Acosta, Oz Anarchy, Mabry Anderson Andrew Arnt, Alison Auditore, Daphne Azzi, Joseph Bliol, Darcy Borg, Jen Bosen, Carolina Caples, Alberto Carbajal, Tara Catalano, Kara Chaddock, Clarie Chilton, Jacey Coca, Norman Davis, Nick Erickson, Jyll Ethier-Mullen, Gale Goldman (aka Goldwoman), Virginia Gott, Joshua Gridley, Aubree Hansen, Stacy Holmstedt, Lathenia Janae, Sandra Johnson Krause, Kiok Jun, Armida Kielty, Jackson Kloog, Igor Kogan, Dani Kump, Peter Kuttner, Myja Lark, Alex Minwoo Lee, Ellen MacEwan, Lamda Mahrya, Teena Maree, Thomas McKee, Max Myers, Ashley Pinnick, Carey Rasmussen, Revolta Art, Maria Rivas, Emily "Stardust" Roberts, Maxine Rose, Bruce Spiegel, Mohan Toopal, Emily Voigt, Kim Walker, Amber Wanlielista, Karla Watson, Mark Watson, Jeff Weiss, Hristi Wilbur, John Woodruff, and Chad Worth. The exhibition will be hung in the studio space at FOUND:RE Phoenix and opens to the public on April 17th. The show is open through October 11th, with an opening reception on April 17th, from 6-8pm. The exhibition will be presented as part of FOUND:RE Contemporary’s regular schedule of rotating exhibitions. The works will be for sale in person and at

rows.Event Name: 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase
rows.Event Description: Get ready for the 22nd Annual Kappa League Scholarship and Awards Showcase - a night of celebrating achievements and supporting education! Kappa League is not just an organization; it's a movement dedicated to empowering young men across the valley. Through mentorship, career opportunities, and exposure, Kappa League instills values of leadership, personal growth, and academic excellence in its members. Our program brings together successful men with 6th – 12th grade male students from diverse backgrounds, nurturing their potential and guiding them towards a brighter future. The Annual Kappa League Showcase is the end-of-year celebration highlighting the accomplishments of the Kappa League Scholars. It serves as an opportunity for the Scholars to showcase skills they've learned during the program. It's also an opportunity to raise funds for the Kappa League program to ensure the programs continued success Da’Shaun Holmes, a proud native of St. Petersburg, FL, is a shining example of what focus, drive, and a passion for learning can accomplish. At The Canterbury School of Florida, he distinguished himself early—excelling academically while actively contributing to organizations such as Mu Alpha Theta and the Science National Honor Society. With a clear vision for his future, Da’Shaun pursued Mechanical Engineering at Florida Atlantic University, where his dedication and ingenuity quickly set him apart. In December 2023, he earned his bachelor’s degree and made history at FAU’s Senior Design Showcase, becoming the first student to win both the People’s Choice Award and the Judge’s Choice Award for his innovative senior project. Before crossing the graduation stage, Da’Shaun was selected for a competitive internship with Honeywell Aerospace in Phoenix, AZ, where he trained as an Advanced Manufacturing Engineer. His performance led to a full-time offer in his hometown—but his ambition didn’t stop there. Recognizing where he could make the greatest impact, Da’Shaun returned to Arizona, earning a promotion and continuing to excel in his engineering career. Today, Da’Shaun not only stands as a rising leader in aerospace engineering but also as a proud member of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc., embracing excellence, achievement, and service in everything he pursues

High energy warehouse event featuring top house and EDM DJ's, dancers, fire performers, and immersive vibes until sunrise. Step into a high energy warehouse experience designed to bring people together through music, movement, and connection. This is a Three stage event featuring a powerful collaboration of nightlife producers, promoters, and DJ's delivering house, EDM, and late-night circuit energy from 10PM until sunrise. The mainstage will deliver the heartbeat of the night, while a transformed party bus stage offers an intimate high intensity dance experience, and a Big Black Bus Stage will provide a karaoke escape to live your best popstar life. Throughout the evening and early morning attendees can expect: Gogo performers Fire performers A fully immersed lighting experience Food and Beverage trucks Pop-up venders Outdoor space This event is about more than muisc. It is about creating a safe, inclusive space where different communities intersect, house heads, ravers, circuit lovers, allies, and newcomers alike. Come dressed to express. Come ready to move. Come ready to connect. This is not just a party. It's a moment. It's new memories made for a lifetime.

rows.Event Name: 9th Arizona School IPM Conference In Person
rows.Event Description: An event for anyone involved or interested in safe and effective pest management in community environments. The conference is a great opportunity for continuing education, professional development and networking for all persons who live and work in community environments (schools and institutions, landscape, turf, parks and rec, and more. This year, we don't have the Indoor and Outdoor tracks. Instead, both days will have talks that apply to all community environments. Choose the day(s) that work best for you. Check out our agendas to make your choices. View agendas here:

rows.Event Name: Coffee at the Capitol + Lunch & Learn: Lawmaker Panel
rows.Event Description: Join ASBA for a full day of small business advocacy at the Arizona State Capitol. ASBA is Arizona’s small business ally at the State Legislature, working to ensure the voice of small business is heard on the issues that matter most. Start the day with Coffee at the Capitol, a casual outdoor gathering where small business owners, community leaders, legislators, and legislative staff can connect during the current legislative session. This free morning event is a great opportunity to build relationships, have meaningful conversations, and engage directly around the policy issues impacting Arizona’s small business community. Complimentary coffee and networking included. In the afternoon, continue the conversation with our Lunch & Learn: Lawmaker Panel, followed by an Advocacy Training led by ASBA’s new CEO, Steve Kaiser, former Arizona State Senator and public policy expert. This ticketed afternoon session will take place indoors and is limited to 40 attendees on a first-come, first-served basis. Lunch is included with the price of admission, and ASBA members receive a reduced ticket price.

Eight storytellers share their most fantastic, bawdy, no holds barred, hilarious, R-rated stories. This local favorite is like no other show Dirty Bits is the Valley's first R-rated live storytelling show, featuring eight-minute curated stories for adults. We are Created and hosted by seasoned storyteller Carly Davis, and Improvisational theater guru Chris Hooper.

rows.Event Name: Healthcare and Business Networking | Elevating Your Potential - Phoenix
rows.Event Description: Strive welcomes you to 'Healthcare and Business Networking',an event for tenured and newcomers at The Grand!Event is 21+. Limited Tickets. Strive welcomes you to 'Healthcare and Business Networking', Phoenix's hottest networking event! We are set in the perfect central location in Downtown for all to join. Are you interested in healthcare and business? Want to elevate your career? Meet Medical Professionals, Consultants, Researchers, Pioneers in the Field, and more who are active in the space or are eager to get started. Pack some business cards and come with an open mind! Meet your future business partners, employers, coworkers, investors, and friends. You don't want to miss this opportunity to expand your network and create a brighter future. We will have a space for our event to check-in. The event will go from 6:00 PM - 8:00 PM(feel free to come at any point in this timeframe). Name tags will be provided. Business casual dress code. Job fair attire. We look forward to meeting you at the event! Sincerely, Strive Networking Team

rows.Event Name: First Friday Activation: Comic Pitch with Damon Begay
rows.Event Description: Share your comics with local artists in a casual cafe setting, get real peer feedback, and leave with notes to improve your work. Meet other local comic artists by sharing your comics in either preproduction or printed. We'll set it up as a casual cafe setting allowing people to pick up your comics to read. Then everyone will be able to leave notes for everyone who submitted comics to be reviewed. By the end of the workshop you'll have real feedback on your comics. Bring you're portfolio and or printed comic!
Fracture Point w/ God Hammered, Buried, Wrath of Eden

rows.Event Name: Pediatric International Trauma Life Support Course (PITLS)
rows.Event Description: Phoenix Children’s Trauma Program is sponsoring a Pediatric ITLS course on May 17, 2026. Phoenix Children’s Trauma Program is sponsoring the upcoming Pediatric ITLS (International Trauma Life Support) course, Sunday, May 17, 2026. This course will focus on the special needs of young trauma patients. In 8 hours, you will learn the principles of proper assessment, management, critical interventions, patient packaging and rapid transport. Practice patient assessment and management on actual “live” children at our skill stations. For more information contact Lisa Dowrick, ldowrick@phoenixchildrens.com or 602-933-3817.
